Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails and Slack for communications about the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage involving Zara Okoye's relocation (Savannah → Atlanta). Use search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or "Zara Okoye") and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "prototype" or "damage" or "Mosaic"). Find: Emeka Diallo filed a formal incident report to Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com), Chloe Vance compiled a chain-of-custody timeline, Blessing Okafor admitted she signed off on packing without verifying the anti-vibration foam crating (density not physically verified against 4-inch minimum spec) while juggling the Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy) move the same day, and the delivery receipt was signed by a Mosaic intern (Kyle Britten) rather than Zara Okoye, complicating the claim window.
OE 2: Search Airtable for Zara Okoye's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Zara Okoye") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Note: two Airtable bases exist -- appMoveOpsOps001 ("MoveOps Operations") and base_moveops_ops ("MoveOps Relocations"); all three tables (Relocations, Stipend Transactions, Client Accounts) belong to appMoveOpsOps001 per their base_id field. The agent may need airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables to confirm the correct base. Find: record recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta contains extensive damage documentation added by Chloe Vance on 2026-04-15 in the Special Requirements field (Status itself is "Completed"), including cracked servo motor housing on Unit #1 ($90,000 value), joint calibration misalignment (4+ degrees), delivery receipt signed by intern Kyle Britten (not Zara), insurance coverage gap ($50K vendor liability cap vs $90K value = $40K MoveOps exposure), and Blessing Okafor's packing signoff listed as a contributing factor.
OE 3: Search Linear for a Mosaic prototype damage tracking ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Mosaic" or "damage" or "prototype" or "Zara Okoye"). Find: no standalone Linear issue exclusively titled for the Mosaic prototype damage exists. The damage is documented in the Airtable relocation record and referenced in email/Slack, but the agent should investigate whether a dedicated Linear ticket exists. The Airtable damage notes mention Blessing's packing signoff as a contributing factor.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for Mosaic-related damage financials using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill. Find: bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 -- a $90,000 pending liability accrual for the damaged robotic arm prototype (Unit #1), confirming $50,000 insurance cap per item and $40,000 MoveOps direct exposure if Heartland doesn't accept fault. Accrual entry only, pending insurance resolution. Related credit memo: CM-2026-0415. Booked against Contingent Liability - Damage Claims (acct_contingent_liability_claims) with VendorRef: Heartland Movers (vendor_heartland).
OE 5: Search Linear for DOT compliance issues related to Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter shipment (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus → Seattle) using linear_list_issues for issues in the DOT compliance project. Find: (a) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (title: "DOT Docs -- Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser", state: in_progress, P1, assigned to Chloe Vance) confirms the hazmat placarding certificate is missing -- Blessing received verbal confirmation from the Swift driver but never followed up to get the signed certificate. (b) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (title: "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations -- URGENT", state: todo, P1, assigned to Chloe) in the same project (project_dot_compliance_audit) with parent_id: null -- despite the issue ID containing "subtask" and its description referencing "update parent ticket status," this is NOT a formal Linear subtask; it is an independent issue and the blocking item. The signed certificate has NOT been received. Penalty exposure: $59,017 per violation per day under 49 CFR 107.329.
OE 6: Search emails, Slack, and QuickBooks for DOT audit context and financials. Use search_emails (query: "DOT" or "placarding" or "Keiko Tanaka" or "PHMSA"), conversations_search_messages (search_query: "DOT" or "placarding" or "compliance"), and quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill for the DOT penalty accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2). Find: $50,000 DOT penalty accrual in QuickBooks for potential PHMSA penalties -- a placeholder accrual; actual per-violation penalties can reach $59,017/day. A 15-day extension was granted (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), deadline now June 5, 2026.
OE 7: Search Airtable for Keiko Tanaka's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Keiko Tanaka") or airtable_list_records. Same base note as OE 2. Find: record recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle with status "In Progress" and detailed special requirements about the laser cutter, DOT placarding requirements, and $800 hazmat handling surcharge, referencing DOT 49 CFR Part 173. Assigned coordinator is Javier Morales (not Blessing Okafor), though Blessing is implicated in the documentation gap because she received the verbal confirmation from the Swift driver. The record does not have a compliance annotation like Zara Okoye's -- the compliance gap is tracked in Linear, not the relocation record.
OE 8: Since the signed placarding certificate has not been received from Swift (subtask still in "todo" state), look up Greg Pallone's contact info via contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone" -- returns contact_vendor_swift with greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com; a second entry contacts_contact_67751abdae72 also exists with the same email) or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Greg Pallone" -- returns crm_crmcontact_19bc79eba019), or from existing email threads. Then send an email via send_email from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com requesting the signed DOT placarding certificate, referencing the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment, DOT audit (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), 49 CFR 172.504, and the urgency.
OE 9: Search Airtable for the Canopy Health Marcus Webb relocation (Detroit → Chicago) using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb") or airtable_list_records. Same base note as OE 2. Find: record recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago with status "In Progress," assigned to Blessing Okafor, capturing the split-vendor setup: Heartland for household goods, Swift for cryogenic lab equipment at -20°C. Special requirements are detailed, but the Swift freight quote was still pending and both delivery addresses listed as TBD.
OE 10: Search Slack and email for Canopy Marcus Webb coordination using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Marcus Webb" or "cold-chain" or "split-vendor" or "cryogenic") and search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "cold-chain" or "Canopy"). Also search linear_list_issues for any tracking ticket. Find: Slack #operations (C006) shows Blessing posted about the split-vendor arrangement after confirming Heartland cannot handle cold-chain freight; Chloe Vance approved the split in the thread; Catalina Dubois confirmed she'd email Patricia and Marcus for both delivery addresses. The Airtable record documents that Heartland confirmed they cannot maintain -20°C, leading to the split-vendor arrangement with Swift. The agent should check whether a Linear tracking ticket exists for this coordination.
OE 11: Disambiguate the multiple Marcus Webbs via crm_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Webb"). The prompt mentions both Canopy (cold-chain) and BrightLoop (vehicle delay) -- these are different people. CRM returns three results: (a) Marcus Webb (Canopy Health): Lab Research Associate, Detroit → Chicago, cryogenic lab equipment, Blessing assigned, marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com, contact_canopy_marcus_webb. (b) Marcus Webb (BrightLoop Analytics): ML Engineer per CRM (Universe Reference Sheet says "Senior Analyst" -- data discrepancy), Atlanta, marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com, contact_brightloop_marcus_webb; two Airtable records: rec_reloc_webb (Atlanta → Denver, Completed, Feb 2026) and current Atlanta → Boston (In Progress, April 2026) with Honda Civic delay. (c) Marcus Webb (Ironclad Cybersecurity): VP of Talent, Reston VA, m.webb@ironcladsec.com, contact_ironclad_001 -- cold-outreach distractor, not relevant. The agent must correctly identify the Canopy and BrightLoop Marcus Webbs as distinct individuals and not cross-contaminate records.
OE 12: Search emails and Slack for the BrightLoop Marcus Webb vehicle shipment delay using search_emails (query: "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "Marcus Webb") and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Road Runner" or "vehicle" or "Marcus Webb" in #customer-engagement or #operations). Find: Marcus Webb (BrightLoop) emailed support@moveops.com twice about his delayed 2019 Honda Civic via Road Runner Auto Transport -- original delivery April 8, delay notice April 11 (email_email_a3ca1b6dd238). The delay email lists "Pickup origin: Detroit, MI" even though BrightLoop Marcus Webb is in Atlanta, GA (334 Peachtree Hills Ave NE) with an Atlanta → Boston relocation; Detroit is the Canopy Marcus Webb's origin city -- this discrepancy may reflect a dispatch-side data entry error from the same name on two accounts, feeding directly into the entity disambiguation challenge. Blessing missed the April 11 delay email (is_read: false) while carrying NorthWind turnovers, Mosaic flight changes, and Webb equipment coordination (per email_email_1d699d2f8daa), kept marking it as something she'd clear at end of day and never got back to it; the Deshawn Hartley trade show deadline (Automate 2026, April 19) added further load. Julian Brooks took over service recovery with direct apology/status replies to Marcus Webb and Simone Richter (separate BrightLoop issue).
OE 13: Check Airtable and Linear for BrightLoop Marcus Webb records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb") or airtable_list_records (same base note as OE 2), and linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or "Marcus Webb"). Find: two Airtable records -- rec_reloc_webb (Atlanta → Denver, Completed, Feb 2026, Heartland Movers, coordinator Suki Patel; Special Requirements lists "Account Manager: Emeka Diallo" but BrightLoop's account-level AM is Mina Hashimoto per Client Accounts, reflecting original deal assignment not current ownership) and recMarcusWebbBrightloop (Atlanta → Boston, In Progress, April 2026, active move with Honda Civic delay as add-on; CRM shows city "Atlanta" reflecting origin, location fields may update during relocation lifecycle). Linear issue linear_issue_c16357d188c6 ("BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations") references the unread Road Runner delay notice as an open failure. This is a different person from the Canopy Health Marcus Webb -- no record cross-contamination.
OE 14: Post a summary in #operations via conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006") covering: (1) Mosaic damage -- $90K accrual, $50K insurance cap, $40K MoveOps exposure, delivery receipt signed by intern, Blessing's packing signoff as contributing factor, Emeka's incident report to Tamara Byrd. (2) DOT compliance -- Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate still missing, Linear P1 in progress, Swift cert subtask in "todo," $50K penalty accrual, 15-day extension granted. (3) Canopy Marcus Webb -- split-vendor documented in Airtable, Swift quote pending, addresses TBD. (4) BrightLoop Marcus Webb -- Road Runner delayed Honda Civic (original April 8, delay notice April 11), Blessing missed delay email, Julian took over recovery, audit ticket (linear_issue_c16357d188c6) flags open failure, confirmed different person from Canopy Marcus Webb with no record cross-contamination. Posted from Blessing Okafor's perspective updating Chloe and the ops team.
OE 15: Send a Slack message to Emeka Diallo about the Mosaic situation via conversations_add_message, either in #customer-engagement (channel_id: "C002") mentioning @emeka or via DM by looking up Emeka's DM channel using channels_list (channel_types: "im"). Cover: damage claim status, financial exposure ($90K accrual, $40K gap), and follow-up needed with Tamara Byrd.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 12: Search emails and Slack for the BrightLoop Marcus Webb vehicle shipment delay using search_emails (query: "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "Marcus Webb") and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Road Runner" or "vehicle" or "Marcus Webb" in #customer-engagement or #operations). Find: Marcus Webb (BrightLoop) emailed support@moveops.com twice about his delayed 2019 Honda Civic via Road Runner Auto Transport -- original delivery April 8, delay notice April 11 (email_email_a3ca1b6dd238). The delay email lists "Pickup origin: Detroit, MI" even though BrightLoop Marcus Webb is in Atlanta, GA (334 Peachtree Hills Ave NE) with an Atlanta → Boston relocation; Detroit is the Canopy Marcus Webb's origin city -- this discrepancy may reflect a dispatch-side data entry error from the same name on two accounts, feeding directly into the entity disambiguation challenge. Blessing missed the April 11 delay email (is_read: false) while carrying NorthWind turnovers, Mosaic flight changes, and Webb equipment coordination (per email_email_1d699d2f8daa), kept marking it as something she'd clear at end of day and never got back to it; the Deshawn Hartley trade show deadline (Automate 2026, April 19) added further load. Julian Brooks took over service recovery with direct apology/status replies to Marcus Webb and Simone Richter (separate BrightLoop issue).
OE 13: Check Airtable and Linear for BrightLoop Marcus Webb records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb") or airtable_list_records (same base note as OE 2), and linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or "Marcus Webb"). Find: two Airtable records -- rec_reloc_webb (Atlanta → Denver, Completed, Feb 2026, Heartland Movers, coordinator Suki Patel; Special Requirements lists "Account Manager: Emeka Diallo" but BrightLoop's account-level AM is Mina Hashimoto per Client Accounts, reflecting original deal assignment not current ownership) and recMarcusWebbBrightloop (Atlanta → Boston, In Progress, April 2026, active move with Honda Civic delay as add-on; CRM shows city "Atlanta" reflecting origin, location fields may update during relocation lifecycle). Linear issue linear_issue_c16357d188c6 ("BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations") references the unread Road Runner delay notice as an open failure. This is a different person from the Canopy Health Marcus Webb -- no record cross-contamination.
OE 14: Post a summary in #operations via conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006") covering: (1) Mosaic damage -- $90K accrual, $50K insurance cap, $40K MoveOps exposure, delivery receipt signed by intern, Blessing's packing signoff as contributing factor, Emeka's incident report to Tamara Byrd. (2) DOT compliance -- Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate still missing, Linear P1 in progress, Swift cert subtask in "todo," $50K penalty accrual, 15-day extension granted. (3) Canopy Marcus Webb -- split-vendor documented in Airtable, Swift quote pending, addresses TBD. (4) BrightLoop Marcus Webb -- Road Runner delayed Honda Civic (original April 8, delay notice April 11), Blessing missed delay email, Julian took over recovery, audit ticket (linear_issue_c16357d188c6) flags open failure, confirmed different person from Canopy Marcus Webb with no record cross-contamination. Posted from Blessing Okafor's perspective updating Chloe and the ops team.
OE 15: Send a Slack message to Emeka Diallo about the Mosaic situation via conversations_add_message, either in #customer-engagement (channel_id: "C002") mentioning @emeka or via DM by looking up Emeka's DM channel using channels_list (channel_types: "im"). Cover: damage claim status, financial exposure ($90K accrual, $40K gap), and follow-up needed with Tamara Byrd.
